source.title: Coherent theta oscillations and reorganization of spike timing in the hippocampal-prefrontal network upon learning

confidence_rationale: High source quality (Neuron, simultaneous HPC-PFC recordings). Strong mapping fidelity for MECH-163: the paper directly demonstrates that HPC-PFC theta coherence peaks at choice points (commitment boundary analogue) after learning, and that the resulting cell assemblies are selectively replayed during sleep. Transfer risk moderate: rodent Y-maze spatial rule learning.
failure_signatures: HPC-PFC theta coherence peaks at choice points specifically, not during continuous running between choice points.
The coherence effect is strongest after rule acquisition — it marks learned rather than novel decisions.

mapping.source_claim_statement: HPC-mPFC theta coherence peaks at maze choice points, most strongly after task rule acquisition. During high-coherence episodes, mPFC pyramidal neurons reorganise their theta phase, synchronous cell assemblies emerge, and these assemblies are preferentially replayed during subsequent slow-wave sleep. Dopamine modulates coherence magnitude.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-163 requires multi-step hippocampal planning signals to communicate with prefrontal commitment circuitry. Benchenane et al. show the biological mechanism: theta coherence couples HPC and mPFC specifically at commitment boundaries (choice points), and this coupling is strongest when the task is learned (when trajectories are policy-relevant). In REE terms, this is the coupling between hippocampal trajectory proposals (ARC-018 rollout) and E3 commitment circuitry. The theta coherence peak at choice points = the commitment boundary event in which hippocampal proposals are broadcast to the prefrontal decision stage. The selective SWR replay of high-coherence assemblies confirms that the trajectory proposals evaluated at commitment boundaries are the ones preferentially consolidated. The dopamine modulation connects to MECH-075 (BG dopaminergic gain): VTA dopamine gates the strength of this HPC-PFC coupling.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper is rodent, Y-maze spatial rule learning. The mapping from spatial choice points to REE's abstract commitment boundaries requires inference. MECH-163's multi-step planning claim requires more than a coupling at a single choice point — the multi-step trajectory structure of the planning signal is not resolved at the cellular level in this paper. The mPFC in rodents is not a straightforward analogue to human PFC — the homology is approximate.
mapping.source_context: Rat simultaneous HPC-mPFC tetrode recording during Y-maze rule reversal learning. LFP coherence analysis, spike-phase coupling, cross-structure assembly analysis, post-task sleep recording.

confidence_components.notes: Strong electrophysiology. Mapping fidelity good for commitment-boundary coupling but incomplete for multi-step trajectory structure. Rodent mPFC-human PFC homology adds transfer risk. Dopamine-coherence coupling provides useful link to MECH-075.
